segments.text: Hello.
Today I'd like to talk about mind loops.
Mind loops are self-reinforcing thoughts and feelings.
I guess you could have good mind loops, but I'm talking today about bad mind loops.
Ones that make you feel bad,
And then you think more about it.
And then you feel bad.
And then you think more about it.
And then you feel bad.
And it just keeps going.
And sometimes when you think about feeling bad and that you realize you're in a mind loop, then you feel bad about feeling bad.
And it just continues.
This past week I've been trying to practice a technique to try to escape those mine loops.
Not escape.
No, no, no.
Escape is the opposite of how this technique works.
I think what it does is it makes these mind loops that keep going over and over again that could be anxiety or fear or shame or there's a number of bad feelings, violence.
These mind loops by doing
this thing, I think what happens is that mind loop just implodes into nothingness.
And I wanted to talk about this technique, but I wanted to explain how I came here, how I got to this point.
And it's actually related to that video I made about quitting neurosurgery

So in that video, I explained why I wanted to quit neurosurgery and all the issues that I was thinking about in terms of medical issues, health care issues, people's health, helping people understand that they can improve their own health.
They have a lot of power to improve their own health.
And that, you know, the health care system does help, but a lot of times it can hurt too.
But the part that I neglected to mention in that video was there was another reason why I left medicine.
It was related in a way to the other video.
But I was stuck in a mind loop.
I was stuck in a mind loop of fear of the future and part of me wanted to be stuck in that mind loop.
So let me explain.
I had mentioned in another, actually a couple, two other videos, about my fear of collapse.
Now collapse is the idea that the
civilization we live in is deteriorating gradually or quickly and that our future conditions of living will be worse than they are today.
That could be from any number of things.
You know, overpopulation, crowding, environmental stuff.
I mean, there's a big fire.
Oh, you can actually see it.
There's a big, there's a mountain right there.
And then there's a big cloud just to the left that looks different than all the other clouds.
All the other clouds are white.
There's a red cloud, slightly red pinkish cloud over there.
That's a huge fire.
And the old me would have looked at that red, that slightly red cloud.
and just like went right into this mind loop of fear of the future and despair and lack of hope thoughts of destruction so over the last several years I I joined the collapse subreddit I don't really
recommend that to most people.
There's a subreddit called r slash collapse.
And it talks all about this, like everything you could possibly want to know about collapse.
And boy, that is a rabbit hole.
I went down and I learned a lot.
This actually was very helpful, lots of information, but it just reinforced this fear cycle, this thoughts of fear of the future.

and not accepting the present because I thought the present was leading to that awful future and so there's no way we can accept or no way I can accept this present because it's going to go to that future even though the future doesn't exist yet and we don't know what the future is but there's this loop that I kept
I kept being in where I would research about climate change, research about earth temperature, sea surface temperatures.
I'd read about forest fires and microplastic pollution and forever chemicals and then overpopulation, migration and have we overshot our population limits?
with the amount of food that we have?

These are the thoughts that I was spending my free time after work, which was a busy job as a neurosurgeon.
I was spending my free time reading about these

very heavy topics that they can induce a lot of anxiety and depression and they did, but actually I was probably already anxious and depressed and feeling hopeless because of the, well, for a number of reasons.

I'm not saying that won't happen, I'm just saying that you don't have to fear it, and I'll explain how.

I wanted to feel bad about the future, because I was already feeling bad.

At the moment, I didn't think it.

then I didn't have to go to my job anymore.
